principle: the product is fact-SUPPRESSION: our value is the rarest, recomputable error rate. A false positive on an established company is a lawsuit; the bar is near-zero on HELD-OUT legit.

LEGIT_in_list.note: gate's veto knows these — near-0 expected, NOT the real test

LEGIT_heldout.note: THE REAL TEST — gate has never seen these. This FP rate is the product metric.

BAD_lookalike.note: FN (missed bad actor) is forgivable; FP is not. But if FN~1.0 we catch nobody.

verdict: READY-ish: held-out gated FP rate near-zero — safe to publish flags conservatively

GRADE.graded_by: Fable recount on raw bytes (independent of builder)

intervals.gated_FP_heldout: 0/115 · Jeffreys 95% [0%, 2.16%] · defensible claim = at most ~2.2%, NEVER bare 0%
intervals.FN_lookalikes: 16/120 = 13.3% · CI [8.1%, 20.3%] · gate leaves FN UNCHANGED (a veto gate suppresses flags, never adds catches — feature with a cost)

GRADE.published_claim: On 115 held-out legitimate companies our verification gate published zero false risk flags (0/115; 95% CI upper bound 2.2%), vetoing every false positive the raw model proposed — including flags it tried to raise on eBay, Razorpay, and Chewy. The gate is deliberately conservative: it suppresses false accusations but does not improve detection, and currently misses 13.3% of lookalike scam domains (16/120; CI 8.1–20.3%).
caveats: held-out-ness ASSERTED not verifiable from these bytes (only 6-entity sample enumerated) — pre-commit the held-out list hash next harness run
verbatim chain-of-custody is the builder's claim (hashes bind the drops, not the _local_only originals)
signer(FP)/verifier(deploy) are both 0x5dF2b0 (9ee735ef lineage) — grader_distinct_from_deployer asserted in-file, deployer unnamed; cured by Umbra or a First-Coin key re-signing
